title: Kristian Kersting
text: Home * People * Kristian Kersting Kristian Kersting [1] Kristian Kersting , a German computer scientist and professor at Computer Science Department, TU Darmstadt and head of its Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AIML) lab [1] . He received his Ph.D. from University of Freiburg on inductive logic programming in 2006 under advisor Luc De Raedt , and had appointments at MIT , Fraunhofer IAIS , University of Bonn , and TU Dortmund . His main research interests are in statistical relational learning and artificial intelligence , and deep learning . Contents 1 CrazyAra 2 Selected Publications 2.1 2000 ... 2.2 2010 ... 2.3 2020 ... 3 External Links 4 References CrazyAra [ edit ] Along with Johannes Fürnkranz at TU Darmstadt, Kristian Kersting led the Deep Learning: Methods and Architectures course in 2018, and supported his students Johannes Czech , Moritz Willig and Alena Beyer in developing the deep learning chess variant engine CrazyAra , which won a five game Crazyhouse online-match versus Justin Tan aka JannLee with 4-1 [2] [3] . Subsequently, he worked with his students Johannes Czech and Patrick Korus on an improvement of AlphaZero's MCTS / PUCT algorithm considering transpositions , optionally applied in CrazyAra. The proposed Monte-Carlo Graph Search uses a directed acyclic graph (DAG) rather than a tree structure [4] . Selected Publications [ edit ] [5] [6] 2000 ...
